title: AST Motor Vehicle Accident: Glennallen [AK25093314]
summary: On 9/13/2025 at approximately 1451 hours, the Alaska State Troopers responded to a report of two juveniles on dirt bikes colliding into each other on Larkspur Loop Road. Both juveniles sustained injuries, and one was transported to the hospital.

title: AST Motor Vehicle Accident: Glennallen [AK25093209]
summary: On 9/13/2025 at approximately 0640 hours, the Alaska State Troopers received a report of a vehicle hitting a moose near mile 65 of the Richardson Highway. The vehicle had minor damage, and no injuries were reported. The moose was deceased and given to a charity.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

entities.address_detail: mile 65 of the Richardson Highway

title: ICT NEWSCAST: Traditional knowledge at the center of salmon restoration, Coharie tribe’s fight for desegregation is personal, rugby and more …
summary: The ICT Newscast for Friday, September 12, 2025, features stories of strong women, Indigenous knowledge, and cultural resilience. title: AST Death Investigation: Fairbanks [AK25092921]
summary: On 9/12/2026, Troopers responded to a death investigation on Birch Lane, Fairbanks. Kinlee Hawkins was found unresponsive in her living room, despite lifesaving efforts by EMS she was pronounced deceased. Investigation is still on going at this time no foul play is suspected.

title: AST DV Assault 4: Wasilla [AK25093159]
summary: On 09/13/2025 at approximately 0014 hours, AST received a report from Sharley Novak that she was driving on Knik Goose Bay Road with a romantic partner. The investigation revealed that while driving Sharley assaulted her romantic partner. Sharley was arrested without incident, transported and remanded to Matsu Pretrial for DV Assault 4, AS11.41.230(a)(1).
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: One-year seat Borough Assembly Candidate Forum hosted by KSTK and Wrangell Sentinel
summary: Borough Assembly candidates run for one seat in this year’s municipal election. They participated in a forum on Sept. 11 at KSTK. From left: Brittani Robbins, Phillip Mach and David Wilson. (Colette Czarnecki/KSTK) KSTK’s Colette Czarnecki and the Wrangell Sentinel’s Jonathan Dawe interviewed the contested borough assembly candidates seeking the one-year term on Sept. 11. Incumbents Brittani Robbins and Phillip Mach sat with challenger David Wilson at KSTK and spoke about their priorities and views. Opening statements Phillip Mach I’m Phillip Mach. I’ve been a resident here in Wrangell for about two and a half years almost. My background is in the seafood industry. I have been fishing since I was 12-years-old with my dad, and after college, kind of got into the processing and management side of things and really enjoy that. So that’s what brought me here to Wrangell. And like a lot of people say, when you get here, you really fall in love with the place. So that’s kind of what got me e...

title: Trump administration terminates University of Alaska grants for Alaska Native, Indigenous students
summary: The sign at the entrance to the University of Alaska Fairbanks campus welcomes students on Sept. 20, 2023.
